questions.question: What is the primary psychological problem with a standard 'feature dump' demo?

options.text: It takes too long to load
Cognitive Overload: The prospect is processing too much visual data to hear your words
The prospect doesn't like software
The colors are distracting

questions.explanation: When you share your screen, the prospect's brain is flooded with visual noise (buttons, sidebars). If you speak while clicking without context, they miss the point due to 'Cognitive Overload.'

questions.question: In the Tell-Show-Tell framework, what is the purpose of the first TELL?

options.text: To ask for the sale
To act as a 'Cognitive Spotlight' that tells the brain exactly what to look for
To stall for time while the page loads
To introduce yourself

questions.explanation: The first TELL primes the brain. By saying 'I'm going to show you how to automate reporting,' you direct their attention away from the distractions and toward the relevant feature.

questions.question: What is 'Mouse Hygiene'?

options.text: Cleaning your actual mouse device
Moving the mouse constantly to keep energy high
Parking the mouse completely still while talking to reduce visual noise
Using a red laser pointer effect

questions.explanation: Amateurs wiggle the mouse nervously. Pros 'park' the mouse. Motion attracts the eye; if the mouse is moving while you talk, they are watching the cursor, not listening to you.

questions.question: What is the final step of the Tell-Show-Tell loop?

options.text: The Tie-Down (Tell/Ask)
Clicking the next button
Refreshing the page
Checking your email

questions.explanation: After showing the feature, you must 'Close the Loop' with a Tie-Down like 'How much time would that save you?' This converts the feature back into business value.

questions.question: Why is 'Signposting' critical in a demo with multiple features?

options.text: It proves you have a lot of features
It creates 'Micro-Closures' so the prospect clearly knows when one topic ends and the next begins
It takes up time
It allows you to read your notes

questions.explanation: Without signposts (transitions), the demo feels like one long, confusing run-on sentence. Signposting allows the prospect to mentally categorize each 'win' before moving on.
